summary: bsc-sdk lookalike harvests wallet, SSH, and secret env vars to hardcoded IP on install
details: @wagni_bot/bsc-sdk 1.1.3 declares itself an "Unofficial bsc-sdk SDK" (impersonating a Binance Smart Chain SDK) but ships no library functionality — its only behavior is the declared postinstall script (postinstall.js), which runs automatically on `npm install`. On install the script walks the user's home directory and crypto-wallet paths (.ethereum,.solana,.bitcoin, ~/Library/Ethereum) looking for files matching wallet/keystore/seed/mnemonic/private/secret/UTC-- and PEM/SSH key patterns, reads private keys from ~/.ssh/, scans file contents for Ethereum/BTC private-key and mnemonic strings, and collects process.env values whose names match PRIVATE/SECRET/TOKEN/KEY/PASSWORD/MNEMONIC/SEED/WALLET/AWS. All collected material is POSTed over plain HTTP to a hardcoded bare-IP endpoint at http://107.161.90.180:7777. The name choice targets developers who handle exactly the crypto wallet and key material the script harvests.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
